claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Let me first read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information, and then I'll ask you some questions about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children you're responsible for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know you have Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you.

To continue assessing your eligibility, could you please tell me: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

Just to confirm a few more details: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Are you responsible for Alex's care - meaning you either live with them (which you've confirmed) or pay towards their upkeep such as food, clothes, or other necessities?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all their care - food, clothes, everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for Alex's care and pay for their upkeep
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the official UK government guidance, you meet all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old (currently 8)
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you and you're responsible for their care
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. Please note that only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, so make sure to submit your application.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for their care, including paying for food, clothes, and all necessities. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they'll usually be responsible for a child if they live with them. All these criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the core requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16, they live together, the claimant is responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for their care, including paying for food, clothes, and all necessities. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they'll usually be responsible for a child if they live with them. All these criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the core requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16, they live together, the claimant is responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.
